channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Sub-second spin and lifetime-limited optical coherences in <sup>171</sup>Yb<sup>3+</sup>:CaWO<sub>4</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70534-9

The authors identify 171Yb3+:CaWO4 as a promising material for quantum technological applications, revealing lifetime-limited optical coherence of 0.75 ms and spin coherence of 0.15 s, highlighting its potential for quantum memories and transducers.
item.dc:title: Sub-second spin and lifetime-limited optical coherences in <sup>171</sup>Yb<sup>3+</sup>:CaWO<sub>4</sub>
item.dc:creator: Alexey Tiranov
Emanuel Green
Sophie Hermans
Erin Liu
Federico Chiossi
Diana Serrano
Pascal Loiseau
Achuthan Manoj Kumar
Sylvain Bertaina
Andrei Faraon
Philippe Goldner

item.title: High-speed energy-efficient memristor confined in sub-5 nm space with elemental oxygen reservoir layer
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70806-4

The switching behavior of oxide-based memristors is governed by uncontrolled formation and rupture of conductive filaments. Here Li et al. report a device with ultrathin elemental oxygen reservoir layer to regulate the random migration of oxygen vacancy, enabling switching speed of ~10 ns and high energy efficiency.
item.dc:title: High-speed energy-efficient memristor confined in sub-5 nm space with elemental oxygen reservoir layer
item.dc:creator: Chenfei Li
Wencheng Niu
Da Wan
Lin Tang
Zhengdao Xie
Kai Zhang
Yuan Liu
Qi Liu
Lei Liao
Xuming Zou
Xingqiang Liu

item.title: Ultrathin Quarter-Waveplates Based on Two-Dimensional Anisotropic NbOCl<sub>2</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70788-3

Miniaturized waveplates are essential for integrated photonics but faced challenges like weak optical anisotropy and scattering losses at small scales. Here, authors demonstrate ultrathin NbOCl2 waveplates with strong optical anisotropy, enabling high-fidelity polarization control at sub-micron thickness.
item.dc:title: Ultrathin Quarter-Waveplates Based on Two-Dimensional Anisotropic NbOCl<sub>2</sub>
item.dc:creator: Jia Gao
Caokun Wang
Chorng Haur Sow
Qi Zhang
Eng Tuan Poh
